span.p: No - NULL is the initial value and app will always write it

span.p: Add NOT NULL column to a live table

span.p: Does the column need a non-trivial default value that must be backfilled?

display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ... NOT NULL DEFAULT literal - instant in PG11+

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Add as nullable first, then backfill, then set NOT NULL

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Step 1 - ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN new_col TYPE NULL

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Step 2 - backfill in batches - see backfill rule

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Step 3 - ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT NOT NULL NOT VALID
